mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based demand expansion, signaling robust consumer confidence and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 10 units and PAYEMS rises > 50k in the same month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-recession recovery phases signals supply-side normalization and capacity utilization recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth rates show no correlation with 5-day SPX returns across multiple recovery episodes, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent demonstrates strong aggregate demand, expanding earnings expectations for S&P 500 constituents and driving positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows Rank IC below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or forward returns in high-GDP-growth quarters are not significantly positive relative to low-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with contracting industrial output signals stagflationary pressures, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 2.0% YoY while INDPRO falls > 1.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where inflation rises and industrial production contracts, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income and consumer spending flows, providing a steady upward bias to equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails to produce positive forward returns in more than 60% of consecutive positive-payroll months, or rolling 12-month predictive accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly rising retail sales and credit demand in a high-interest-rate environment indicates economic overheating, prompting anticipated monetary tightening that dampens equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the overheating signal, or forward returns show no significant difference from baseline when controlling for GDP growth.
